revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 乙宮神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_tatekushi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町立串郷628 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|05|04.3|N|129|06|31.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 彦火々出見尊命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =11月10日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（11月9、10日） }}
'''乙宮神社'''（おとみやじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]立串（たてくし）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[ホオリ|彦火々出見尊命]]を主祭神に祀る。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[元和]]6年([[1620年]])、[[柴田勝家]]第2の孫である勝厚が当地に居住したと伝えられ、家運長久のために1社（祭神は不明）を建立したとされる。以降、次第に住民が増加するに従い、勝厚の社を地域の氏神として崇敬し、[[明暦]]3年（[[1657年]]）9月に社殿を建立して「'''乙宮大明神'''」の神号を奉じ奉祀した。 [[ファイル:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] [[明治]]3年([[1867年]]）5月5日に社号を「乙宮神社」に改称した。[[村社]]に列せられ、[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）10月18日には[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定されている。
== 祭祀 == 11月9、10日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[元旦祭]]（1月1日） *[[厄払い]]（1月） *[[例大祭]]（11月9、10日） *[[大祓式]]（12月31日）
== 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|境内社]] *[[八坂神社]] *[[恵美須神社]] *竈神社 *[[稲荷神社]] *仇子神社
== その他の神社 == その他の立串郷の神社に'''琴平神社'''、大瀬良地区・'''山神社'''、小瀬良地区・'''塩釜神社'''がある。
また隣接する小串郷に[[小串神社]]が、津和崎郷には[[本山神社 (新上五島町)|本山神社]]がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_tate3.JPG|琴平神社 画像: IMG_tate4.JPG|塩釜神社 画像: IMG_kogushi1.JPG|小串神社（小串郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著
{{DEFAULTSORT:おとみやしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 乙宮神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_tatekushi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町立串郷628 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|05|04.3|N|129|06|31.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 彦火々出見尊命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =11月10日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（11月9、10日） }}
'''乙宮神社'''（おとみやじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]立串（たてくし）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[ホオリ|彦火々出見尊命]]を主祭神に祀る。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[元和]]6年([[1620年]])、[[柴田勝家]]第2の孫である勝厚が当地に居住したと伝えられ、家運長久のために1社（祭神は不明）を建立したとされる。以降、次第に住民が増加するに従い、勝厚の社を地域の氏神として崇敬し、[[明暦]]3年（[[1657年]]）9月に社殿を建立して「'''乙宮大明神'''」の神号を奉じ奉祀した。 [[ファイル:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] [[明治]]3年([[1867年]]）5月5日に社号を「乙宮神社」に改称した。[[村社]]に列せられ、[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）10月18日には[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定されている。
== 祭祀 == 11月9、10日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[元旦祭]]（1月1日） *[[厄払い]]（1月） *[[例大祭]]（11月9、10日） *[[大祓式]]（12月31日）
== 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|境内社]] *[[八坂神社]] *[[恵美須神社]] *竈神社 *[[稲荷神社]] *仇子神社
== その他の神社 == その他の立串郷の神社に'''琴平神社'''、大瀬良地区・'''山神社'''、小瀬良地区・'''塩竈神社'''がある。
また隣接する小串郷に'''[[小串神社]]'''が、津和崎郷には'''[[本山神社 (新上五島町)|本山神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_tate3.JPG|琴平神社 画像: IMG_tate4.JPG|塩竈神社 画像: IMG_kogushi1.JPG|小串神社（小串郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著
{{DEFAULTSORT:おとみやしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 乙宮神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_tatekushi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町立串郷628 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 彦火々出見尊命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月10日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（11月9、10日） }}
'''乙宮神社'''（おとみやじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]]立串郷（たてくしごう）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[ホオリ|彦火々出見尊命]]を主祭神に祀る。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）、[[柴田勝家]]第2の孫である勝厚が当地に居住したと伝えられ、家運長久のために1社（祭神は不明）を建立したとされる。以降、次第に住民が増加するに従い、勝厚の社を地域の氏神として崇敬し、[[明暦]]3年（[[1657年]]）9月に社殿を建立して「'''乙宮大明神'''」の神号を奉じ奉祀した。
[[明治]]3年（[[1867年]]）5月5日に社号を「乙宮神社」に改称した。[[村社]]に列せられ、[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）10月18日には[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定されている。
== 祭祀 == 11月9、10日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[元旦祭]]（1月1日） *[[厄払い]]（1月） *[[例大祭]]（11月9、10日） *[[大祓式]]（12月31日）
== 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|境内社]] *八坂神社 *恵美須神社 *竈神社 *稲荷神社 *仇子神社
== その他の神社 == その他の立串郷の神社に'''琴平神社'''、大瀬良地区・'''山神社'''、小瀬良地区・'''塩竈神社'''がある。
また隣接する小串郷に'''[[小串神社]]'''が、津和崎郷には'''[[本山神社 (新上五島町)|本山神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_tate3.JPG|琴平神社 画像: IMG_tate4.JPG|塩竈神社 画像: IMG_kogushi1.JPG|小串神社（小串郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著
{{DEFAULTSORT:おとみやしんしや}} [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 乙宮神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_tatekushi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町立串郷628 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 彦火々出見尊命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月10日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（11月9、10日） }}
'''乙宮神社'''（おとみやじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]]立串郷（たてくしごう）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[ホオリ|彦火々出見尊命]]を主祭神に祀る。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）、[[柴田勝家]]第2の孫である勝厚が当地に居住したと伝えられ、家運長久のために1社（祭神は不明）を建立したとされる。以降、次第に住民が増加するに従い、勝厚の社を地域の氏神として崇敬し、[[明暦]]3年（[[1657年]]）9月に社殿を建立して「'''乙宮大明神'''」の神号を奉じ奉祀した。
[[明治]]3年（[[1867年]]）5月5日に社号を「乙宮神社」に改称した。[[村社]]に列せられ、[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）10月18日には[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定されている。
== 祭祀 == 11月9、10日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[元旦祭]]（1月1日） *[[厄払い]]（1月） *[[例大祭]]（11月9、10日） *[[大祓式]]（12月31日）
== 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|境内社]] *八坂神社 *恵美須神社 *竈神社 *稲荷神社 *仇子神社
== その他の神社 == その他の立串郷の神社に'''琴平神社'''、大瀬良地区・'''山神社'''、小瀬良地区・'''塩竈神社'''がある。
また隣接する小串郷に'''[[小串神社]]'''が、津和崎郷には'''[[本山神社 (新上五島町)|本山神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_tate3.JPG|琴平神社 画像: IMG_tate4.JPG|塩竈神社 画像: IMG_kogushi1.JPG|小串神社（小串郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著
{{DEFAULTSORT:おとみやしんしや}} [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Otomiya Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tatekushi.JPG|250px]] | Location = Shinkamigoto, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} | Deity = Hoori | Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = November 10 | Rituals = Kamigoto Kagura (November 9, 10) }}
'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigoto|qq=}}, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}} as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna (Japan)|qq=}} 6 ({{ill|1620|ja|1620th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1620|qq=}}), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|ja|柴田勝家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Katsuie|qq=}}, settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In {{ill|Meireki|ja|明暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meireki|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1657|ja|1657th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1657|qq=}}) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1867|ja|1867th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1867|qq=}}) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}, and on October 18 of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|1929|ja|1929th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1929|qq=}}), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|qq=}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|qq=}} (January) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|qq=}} (December 31)
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|qq=}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|qq=}}'''.
<gallery> File:IMG_tate3.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tate4.JPG|Shiogama Shrine File:IMG_kogushi1.JPG|Kogushi Shrine (Kogushi district) </gallery>
== References == * ''{{ill|Shingyo Town History|ja|新魚目町郷土誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingyo Town History|qq=}}'' (1986), Shingyo Town History Compilation Committee * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Records|ja|五島神社誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Shrine Records|qq=}}'' (1943, reprint), Shinto Association of Minamimatsuura District {{shinto-stub}}
{{citation needed|ja|乙宮神社|version=39192446|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Shinkamigoto Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Otomiya Shrine (Tategushi, Shinkamigoto)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]]
}}

As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In {{ill|Meireki|ja|明暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meireki|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1657|ja|1657th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1657|qq=}}) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1867|ja|1867th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1867|qq=}}) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}, and on October 18 of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|1929|ja|1929th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1929|qq=}}), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|qq=}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|qq=}} (January) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|qq=}} (December 31)
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|qq=}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|qq=}}'''.

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷) up to rev 85684865
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 乙宮神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_tatekushi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町立串郷628 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 彦火々出見尊命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月10日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（11月9、10日） }}
'''乙宮神社'''（おとみやじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]]立串郷（たてくしごう）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[ホオリ|彦火々出見尊命]]を主祭神に祀る。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）、[[柴田勝家]]第2の孫である勝厚が当地に居住したと伝えられ、家運長久のために1社（祭神は不明）を建立したとされる。以降、次第に住民が増加するに従い、勝厚の社を地域の氏神として崇敬し、[[明暦]]3年（[[1657年]]）9月に社殿を建立して「'''乙宮大明神'''」の神号を奉じ奉祀した。
[[明治]]3年（[[1867年]]）5月5日に社号を「乙宮神社」に改称した。[[村社]]に列せられ、[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）10月18日には[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定されている。
== 祭祀 == 11月9、10日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[元旦祭]]（1月1日） *[[厄払い]]（1月） *[[例大祭]]（11月9、10日） *[[大祓式]]（12月31日）
== 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|境内社]] *八坂神社 *恵美須神社 *竈神社 *稲荷神社 *仇子神社
== その他の神社 == その他の立串郷の神社に'''琴平神社'''、大瀬良地区・'''山神社'''、小瀬良地区・'''塩竈神社'''がある。
また隣接する小串郷に'''[[小串神社]]'''が、津和崎郷には'''[[本山神社 (新上五島町)|本山神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_tate3.JPG|琴平神社 画像: IMG_tate4.JPG|塩竈神社 画像: IMG_kogushi1.JPG|小串神社（小串郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著
{{DEFAULTSORT:おとみやしんしや}} [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Otomiya Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tatekushi.JPG|250px]] | Location = Shinkamigoto, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} | Deity = Hoori | Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = November 10 | Rituals = Kamigoto Kagura (November 9, 10) }}
'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|qq=}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|qq=}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|qq=}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|qq=}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|qq=}}'''.
<gallery> File:IMG_tate3.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tate4.JPG|Shiogama Shrine File:IMG_kogushi1.JPG|Kogushi Shrine (Kogushi district) </gallery>
== References == * ''{{ill|Shingyo Town History|ja|新魚目町郷土誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingyo Town History|qq=}}'' (1986), Shingyo Town History Compilation Committee * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Records|ja|五島神社誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Shrine Records|qq=}}'' (1943, reprint), Shinto Association of Minamimatsuura District {{shinto-stub}}
{{citation needed|ja|乙宮神社|version=39192446|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Otomiya-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11369926]] Wikidata name: Otomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Otomiya Shrine | image = File:IMG tatekushi.JPG | Location = Shinkamigoto, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} | Deity = Hoori | Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = November 10 | Rituals = Kamigoto Kagura (November 9, 10) | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.063111|N|129.107972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|qq=|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|qq=|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
<gallery> File:IMG_tate3.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tate4.JPG|Shiogama Shrine File:IMG_kogushi1.JPG|Kogushi Shrine (Kogushi district) </gallery>
== References == * ''{{ill|Shingyo Town History|ja|新魚目町郷土誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingyo Town History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1986), Shingyo Town History Compilation Committee * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Records|ja|五島神社誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Shrine Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1943, reprint), Shinto Association of Minamimatsuura District {{shinto-stub}}
{{citation needed|ja|乙宮神社|version=39192446|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|qq=|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
<gallery> File:IMG_tate3.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tate4.JPG|Shiogama Shrine File:IMG_kogushi1.JPG|Kogushi Shrine (Kogushi district) </gallery>
== References == * ''{{ill|Shingyo Town History|ja|新魚目町郷土誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingyo Town History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1986), Shingyo Town History Compilation Committee * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Records|ja|五島神社誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Shrine Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1943, reprint), Shinto Association of Minamimatsuura District {{shinto-stub}}

'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]

As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otomiya-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369926]] Wikidata name: Otomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Otomiya Shrine | image = File:IMG tatekushi.JPG | Location = Shinkamigoto, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} | Deity = Hoori | Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = November 10 | Rituals = Kamigoto Kagura (November 9, 10) | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.063111|N|129.107972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
<gallery> File:IMG_tate3.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tate4.JPG|Shiogama Shrine File:IMG_kogushi1.JPG|Kogushi Shrine (Kogushi district) </gallery>
== References == * ''{{ill|Shingyo Town History|ja|新魚目町郷土誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingyo Town History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1986), Shingyo Town History Compilation Committee * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Records|ja|五島神社誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Shrine Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1943, reprint), Shinto Association of Minamimatsuura District {{shinto-stub}}

; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otomiya-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369926]] Wikidata name: Otomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Otomiya Shrine | image = File:IMG tatekushi.JPG | Location = Shinkamigoto, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} | Deity = Hoori | Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = November 10 | Rituals = Kamigoto Kagura (November 9, 10) | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.063111|N|129.107972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." == Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Otomiya-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Otomiya-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otomiya-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369926]] Wikidata name: Otomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Otomiya Shrine | image = File:IMG tatekushi.JPG | Location = Shinkamigoto, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} | Deity = Hoori | Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = November 10 | Rituals = Kamigoto Kagura (November 9, 10) | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.063111|N|129.107972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
<gallery> File:IMG_tate3.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tate4.JPG|Shiogama Shrine File:IMG_kogushi1.JPG|Kogushi Shrine (Kogushi district) </gallery>
== References == * ''{{ill|Shingyo Town History|ja|新魚目町郷土誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingyo Town History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1986), Shingyo Town History Compilation Committee * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Records|ja|五島神社誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Shrine Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1943, reprint), Shinto Association of Minamimatsuura District {{shinto-stub}}

; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Otomiya-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Otomiya-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otomiya-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369926]] Wikidata name: Otomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Otomiya Shrine | image = File:IMG tatekushi.JPG | Location = Shinkamigoto, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|03|47.2|N|129|06|28.7|E|scale:25000}} | Deity = Hoori | Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = November 10 | Rituals = Kamigoto Kagura (November 9, 10) | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.063111|N|129.107972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
<gallery> File:IMG_tate3.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tate4.JPG|Shiogama Shrine File:IMG_kogushi1.JPG|Kogushi Shrine (Kogushi district) </gallery>
== References == * ''{{ill|Shingyo Town History|ja|新魚目町郷土誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingyo Town History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1986), Shingyo Town History Compilation Committee * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Records|ja|五島神社誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Shrine Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1943, reprint), Shinto Association of Minamimatsuura District {{shinto-stub}}

'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] 6 ([[:en:1620|1620]]), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Shibata Katsuie]], settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 3 ([[:en:1657|1657]]) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 ([[:en:1867|1867]]) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 4 ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]

'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto}}, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori}} as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} 6 ({{ill|1620|en|1620|WD=Q6810|lt=1620}}), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|WD=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie}}, settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki}} 3 ({{ill|1657|en|1657|WD=Q6987|lt=1657}}) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}}) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 4 ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}}), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto}}, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori}} as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} 6 ({{ill|1620|en|1620|WD=Q6810|lt=1620}}), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|WD=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie}}, settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki}} 3 ({{ill|1657|en|1657|WD=Q6987|lt=1657}}) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}}) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 4 ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}}), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
<gallery> File:IMG_tate3.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tate4.JPG|Shiogama Shrine File:IMG_kogushi1.JPG|Kogushi Shrine (Kogushi district) </gallery>
== References == * ''{{ill|Shingyo Town History|ja|新魚目町郷土誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingyo Town History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1986), Shingyo Town History Compilation Committee * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Records|ja|五島神社誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Shrine Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1943, reprint), Shinto Association of Minamimatsuura District {{shinto-stub}}

In {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} 6 ({{ill|1620|en|1620|WD=Q6810|lt=1620}}), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|WD=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie}}, settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki}} 3 ({{ill|1657|en|1657|WD=Q6987|lt=1657}}) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}}) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 4 ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}}), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
<gallery> File:IMG_tate3.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tate4.JPG|Shiogama Shrine File:IMG_kogushi1.JPG|Kogushi Shrine (Kogushi district) </gallery>

It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 4 ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}}), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=Apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=Ōharae-shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
