'''Otomiya Shrine''' (乙宮神社, Otomiya Shrine) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, Minamimatsuura District, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan.
== Deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori|1=Hoori}} as the main deity.
== History == [[File:IMG_tate1.JPG|170px|thumb|Main Hall]] The founding date is unknown. In {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)|1=Yuanhe}} 6 ({{ill|1620|en|1620|WD=Q6810|lt=1620|1=1620}}), it is said that Katsunobu, the second grandson of {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|WD=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie|1=Shibata Katsuie}}, settled in the area and established a shrine (the enshrined deity is unknown) for the prosperity of his family. As the population increased, Katsunobu’s shrine came to be revered as the local tutelary deity. In {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki|1=Meireki}} 3 ({{ill|1657|en|1657|WD=Q6987|lt=1657|1=1657}}) September, a shrine building was constructed, and the title "'''Otomiya Daimyōjin'''" was given.
In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867|1=1867}}) May 5, the shrine’s name was changed to "Otomiya Shrine." It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 4 ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}}), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=Apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=Ōharae-shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31) [[ja:乙宮神社 (新上五島町立串郷)]]
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

It was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on October 18 of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 4 ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}}), it was listed as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku Ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Rites == During the annual festival on November 9 and 10, {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed. Kamigoto Kagura is included in the nationally recognized {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, and independently designated as a Nagasaki Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Yakubarai|ja|厄払い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakubarai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=Apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (January) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 9, 10) * {{ill|Ōharai-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharai-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=Ōharae-shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December 31)
== Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:IMG_tate2.JPG|170px|thumb|Auxiliary Shrines]] * Yasaka Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Kamado Shrine * Inari Shrine * Adako Shrine
== Other Shrines == Other shrines in Tatekushi include '''Kotohira Shrine''', '''Yama Shrine''' in Osera district, and '''Shiogama Shrine''' in Kose district.
Additionally, in the neighboring Kogushi district, there is '''{{ill|Kogushi Shrine|ja|小串神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogushi Shrine|1=Kogushi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458779}}''', and in Tsuwazaki district, there is '''{{ill|Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|本山神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
